evidence: BlackLabDummyTest.testSubjectOf() has zero assertions; swallows exceptions silently; just prints to stderr and returns if index missing
RelationsValidationTest uses public static void main() instead of @Test; Maven Surefire will never execute it
DependencySketchTest and BlackLabBcqlConcordanceTest hard-code Windows path D:\\corpora_philsci\\bi; all tests skip silently in CI via Assumptions.assumeTrue or @EnabledIf
WordSketchApiServer.java (REST API), SemanticFieldExplorer.java (key advertised feature), BlackLabSnippetParser.java, SketchGrammarParser.java have zero test coverage
CQLVerifierTest verifies only isMatched()==true for most cases; does not assert which token was matched, at what position, or what the matched lemma was
CQLParserTest has 30+ well-named cases covering labeled positions, negation, OR, AND, distance, regex-vs-field OR disambiguation
LogDiceCalculatorTest includes symmetry property test, monotonicity test, and real-corpus scenario regression tests
LongIntHashMapTest tests collision/rehash with small initial capacity, negative keys, Long.MAX_VALUE
TestGrammarConfig is a utility class with no @Test methods placed in test directory — creates noise

strengths: CQLParserTest is extensive (30+ cases), covers real grammar macro patterns, edge cases like value-level vs field-level OR, and whitespace handling
LogDiceCalculatorTest goes beyond happy-path with symmetry and monotonicity property tests plus real-corpus regression values
LongIntHashMapTest properly exercises collision resolution by constructing the map with a deliberately small initial capacity
SimpleTaggerTest uses @BeforeEach for isolation and tests each linguistic rule (suffix, form, capitalization) individually
PredicatesTest and TokenPatternMatchTest test behavioral contracts cleanly with targeted positive and negative cases
test_strategy.issue_character: The pure-unit layer (parser, scoring, data structures) is tested with care, but the integration layer is represented by non-executable or always-skipping test shells that provide no CI protection.
test_strategy.score_rationale: Unit tests for algorithmic components (CQLParser, LogDice, LongIntHashMap, SimpleTagger, Predicates, TokenWindow) are solid and behaviorally oriented. However, several 'test' files are effectively dead code (a main() method, a no-assertion print loop), and the two most user-visible components — the REST API and SemanticFieldExplorer — have no tests at all. Integration tests for the BlackLab backend are permanently disabled via hardcoded local paths, eliminating any CI signal for the core query pipeline.

issues.summary: BlackLabDummyTest has no assertions and silently swallows all exceptions
evidence: Method body checks `if (!indexDir.exists()) { return; }` — returns silently instead of skipping via Assumptions
Loop catches all exceptions with `System.out.println("ERROR: " + e.getMessage())` — failures are invisible
No `assert*` or `fail()` calls anywhere in the test
issues.suggestion: Either convert to a real parameterized test with assertions on result sizes/content, or delete it. If the intent is exploratory debugging, move it to a separate manual test source set excluded from `mvn test`.

issues.summary: RelationsValidationTest uses main() not @Test — Maven Surefire never runs it
evidence: Class declares `public static void main(String[] args)` with no JUnit annotations
No `@Test` method present; the class will be compiled but never executed by Maven Surefire
issues.suggestion: Convert to a proper JUnit 5 test class: annotate the validation logic with `@Test` and replace `System.exit(1)` with `assertEquals(0, withoutPattern, "Relations missing patterns: " + ...)`.

issues.summary: DependencySketchTest and BlackLabBcqlConcordanceTest always skip in CI due to hardcoded local Windows paths
evidence: DependencySketchTest: `private static final String INDEX_PATH = "D:\\corpora_philsci\\bi";` with `Assumptions.assumeTrue(indexDir.exists(), ...)`
BlackLabBcqlConcordanceTest: same path, `@EnabledIf("indexExists")` where `indexExists()` checks the hardcoded path
The `testDependencyEmptyLemma` and `testDependencyNullDeprel` tests verify pure logic (empty/null input) but still depend on the external index to run
issues.suggestion: Extract the pure-logic edge-case tests (empty lemma, null deprel) into unit tests that mock or stub the executor. For real index tests, read the path from an environment variable or system property (`INDEX_PATH` env var with `Assumptions.assumeTrue(path != null)`).

issues.summary: WordSketchApiServer, SemanticFieldExplorer, and BlackLabSnippetParser have zero test coverage
evidence: No test file found matching WordSketchApiServer or SemanticFieldExplorer
CLAUDE.md lists SemanticFieldExplorer as a key component with single-seed exploration logic
BlackLabSnippetParser parses XML snippets from BlackLab — string parsing logic that is easily unit-testable with fixture strings
issues.suggestion: Add unit tests for BlackLabSnippetParser using hardcoded XML fixture strings. Add at least one integration smoke test for the /api/sketch/{lemma} endpoint using an embedded HTTP server. SemanticFieldExplorer can be tested with a mock QueryExecutor returning fixture collocations.

issues.summary: CQLVerifierTest verifies only isMatched() boolean; matched position and collocate identity are never asserted
evidence: 13 out of 15 test methods only call `assertTrue(result.isMatched(), ...)` or `assertFalse(result.isMatched())`
`testMultiElementPattern` comment says "Should find both 'big' (jj) at position 1 and 'is' (vb) at position 3" but only checks the boolean result
No test verifies `result.getMatchedCollocate()`, matched position, or that the correct token was selected when multiple tokens match
issues.suggestion: Extend assertions to also check the matched token's lemma/position from `VerificationResult`. For `testNounPattern`, assert the matched token is NOT the headword (position 2) to confirm the verifier excludes the head from collocate candidates.

root_causes: Tests appear to have been written in two distinct phases: careful unit tests for algorithmic components (parser, scoring) and exploratory/spike tests for the BlackLab integration layer that were never cleaned up into real assertions
No CI environment appears to have enforced test passage — tests that silently skip or produce no assertions have gone unnoticed
likely_symptoms: Regressions in SemanticFieldExplorer or the REST API go undetected until manual QA
BlackLabSnippetParser XML parsing bugs surface only at runtime against a live corpus
A developer running `mvn test` sees all green but has no confidence in the query pipeline
possible_false_positives: HybridAlgorithmTest is named misleadingly (it tests GrammarConfigLoader, not an algorithm) but the tests themselves are valid and useful
TestGrammarConfig being in the test directory without @Test methods is a style issue, not a test-strategy failure per se
